name: Actinium
appearance: Actinium is a soft, silvery-white radioactive metal. It glows blue in the dark because its intense radioactivity excites the air around it.
uses: Actinium is a very powerful source of alpha rays, but is rarely used outside research.
naturalAbundance: Actinium used for research purposes is made by the neutron bombardment of radium-226. Actinium also occurs naturally in uranium ores.
biologicalRole: Actinium has no known biological role. It is toxic due to its radioactivity.
